item.title: As per photo needs to be done, 7th July

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Sparrows Lane, Eltham

item.title: Tree overgrown onto traffic signal, 7th July
item.description:
Vehicles on C phase approach (only running stage 2 when D phase detectors activated). Vehicle detector pole 9 detection poor due to overgrown foliage. Affecting the traffic signals.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Amhurst Road, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Dead fox on road. lordship lane a2216, 7th July
item.description:
Dead fox on lordship lane near barry road.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 339, Lordship Lane, East Dulwich, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Large green hedge overhanging path, 7th July
item.description:
There is a large green hedge overhanging onto the path making the path very narrow, it’s on the bend and difficult for larger people, those with pushchairs and wheelchairs to pass safely on the pavement
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Pickford Close, West Heath, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Uneven path near posts, 7th July
item.description:
The floor has become damaged and has left the surface very uneven and rough. It’s a trip hazard and very difficult to pass by with a pram.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Pickford Close, West Heath, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Neighbour at No 17 Russell Close DA7 4HX had to bag up a dead fox which is in her bin, causing a stink, 7th July
item.description:
Causing a stench from bin

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Russell Close, Barnehurst, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Overgrown plants and fly tipping area., 7th July
item.description:
This area has a large patch that has just been used as a dumping ground. There are things from tyres, slides, beds, ladders, wardrobes, rubbish, overgrown plants, you name it! It’s is at the end of Carr Grove in the car park/ residential garden (behind the fence of it). There are a family of injured foxes that are there too along with MANY flies!

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Carr Grove, Woolwich
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Over grown plants and trees, 7th July
item.description:
Hey. The overgrown plants and trees are making it very difficult to walk on the pavement. I’m unsure if the business is abandoned or what, by the looks of it is, but this right opposite a nursery and there are a lot of have prams and young children. This is also a residential area and the foot path is blocked- sometimes we must get on the road to get by and then back on the pavement. I’d appreciate it if the appropriate person is reached and these plants could be neatened up so that they’re not causing inconvenience to people. They’re lovely plants but just seem neglected and extremely overgrown. Thank you for your time! Natalie

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Boneta Road, Woolwich
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Sign knocked down, 7th July
item.description:
This sign has been today and close my is a kids school.kid passing everyday to go to schoool

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Northumberland Avenue, Falconwood, Greater London

item.title: Wild Overground bushes growing Gordon team required with trimmer not lawn mower hand maintenance labour.., 6th July

Wild Overground bushes need shimming. Gardening team required hand maintenance labor.

item.title: Overhanging vine branches need trimming outside 22 Daintry way, 6th July
item.description:

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Daintry Way, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet
